diagrams.title: E-Waste Pollutant Pathways to Human Health
diagrams.description: Shows how pollutants from e-waste reach humans through environmental and food chain pathways
elements: Humans exposed through inhalation, ingestion, dermal contact
Health effects shown in human figure (brain, lungs, kidneys, liver)
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the complete exposure pathway from disposal to health effects, helping understand why multiple routes matter

diagrams.title: Food Chain Biomagnification
diagrams.description: Pyramid showing increasing toxin concentration at each trophic level
elements: Arrows showing toxin accumulation increasing upward
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrate how toxins concentrate at higher trophic levels, explaining why top predators and humans face greatest risk

diagrams.title: Heavy Metals and Target Organs
diagrams.description: Human body diagram showing which organs are affected by which heavy metals
diagrams.purpose: Associate each toxic metal with its primary target organ for easier recall

diagrams.title: E-Waste Disposal Hierarchy
diagrams.description: Inverted pyramid showing preferred to least preferred disposal methods
elements: Third: Energy recovery (incineration with energy capture)
diagrams.purpose: Understand the waste management hierarchy and why recycling is prioritized over disposal

visuals.title: Effects Of E Waste Pollutants On Human Health And Environment
visuals.description: Visual diagram for effects of e waste pollutants on human health and environment
